finding: April 11 shows a manic-mode signature: 16 distinct active hours (08:00-23:37 local), 3193 user messages, 25 sessions across 3 projects (jhana, weekend, claude), with 265 late-night messages. This is the most intense day in the 30-day window and matches the manic-mode pattern of short intense bursts with scope escalation.

April 14 shows another manic spike: 17 active hours, 2098 messages, 8 different projects, with work continuing past midnight (00:33 local). The session content shows rapid project-switching between nixos, dotfiles, autodoro, whisper-writer, marketplace, and jonathans-assistant — scope spreading across many fronts in a single day.

Late-night work (after 23:00 local) appears on 5 of 13 active days (Apr 4, 7, 10, 11, 14). On Apr 7, the user was actively debugging hooks and switching between intender and claude projects past midnight. This is a recurring pattern, not a one-off. The user's north star mentions 'present in my body' and 'mindful' as values — late-night screen work may conflict with these.

April 11-14 forms a 4-day intensity streak: 16h/12h/7h/17h active days with 3193/2430/961/2098 messages respectively. There is no visible recovery day between them. Apr 12 involved 7 projects and Apr 14 involved 8 projects. This pattern of sustained high output without breaks matches burnout risk signatures.

April 12 shows a project-scatter pattern: 22 sessions across 7 different projects in 10 hours (08:38-18:50). While the total hours are not extreme, the breadth of context-switching — jhana, weekend, nixos, claude-code, marketplace, claude, weekend — in a single day suggests scope escalation, a manic-mode sub-signal.
